title: gilia latiflora subsp. elongata
intro: Gilia latiflora subsp. elongata, also known as Mexican bluebells, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) that is native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Gilia latiflora subsp. elongata include its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to treat colds, flu, and other upper respiratory infections.
